stories.title: **Mason Miller's 34⅔-Inning Scoreless Streak Ends on Controversial Call**

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Mason Miller's 34⅔-inning scoreless streak is over — and it ended on a blown call. Umpire Dan Merzel called a foul ball fair, giving Matt Shaw an infield hit. The Padres franchise record is his. The all-time reliever record (41 IP, Olson 1988) will have to wait. #Padres

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: The streak is gone, but the numbers remain: 34⅔ scoreless IP, a Padres franchise record, 0.00 ERA, 28 strikeouts. Mason Miller is still elite. The ump just made sure that's how this chapter ends. #Padres #MLB

article.headline: Mason Miller's 34⅔-Inning Scoreless Streak Ends on a Controversial Umpire Call

stories.title: **Spencer Torkelson Ties Detroit Tigers Record With HR in 5 Straight Games**

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
One week ago: Spencer Torkelson was hitting under .200 with ZERO home runs in 2026.

Today: He's homered in 5 straight games, tying the Detroit Tigers franchise record shared by Hank Greenberg, Rudy York, Willie Horton, and Marcus Thames.

Baseball is wild. #Tigers #DetroitTigers

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Spencer Torkelson's next HR sets the all-time Detroit Tigers record for consecutive games with a home run — and would mark the longest such streak in MLB since Rafael Devers in 2024.

The 24-year-old is locked in. #Tigers #DetroitTigers

stories.title: **Red Sox Fire Manager Alex Cora and Five Coaches**

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text: Alex Cora is out. The Red Sox fired their manager — and 5 coaches — with a 10-17 record. Chad Tracy, manager at Triple-A Worcester, is the interim. First mid-season managerial firing for Boston since Jimy Williams in August 2001. #RedSox #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text: Alex Cora won a World Series with the Red Sox in 2018. Players reportedly cried when he was let go. A 10-17 record in 2026 was the final straw. Craig Breslow called it "necessary." Boston's offseason expectations have completely evaporated. #RedSox

article.headline: Red Sox Fire Alex Cora, Name Chad Tracy Interim Manager After 10-17 Start

stories.title: **Atlanta Braves First Team in MLB to Reach 20 Wins**

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: The Atlanta Braves are the first team in MLB to win 20 games in 2026. They went 76-86 last season and missed the playoffs. Walt Weiss has this team playing like a World Series contender. The NL East belongs to Atlanta right now. #Braves #ForTheA

stories.title: **Shohei Ohtani Breaks Out — 3 Hits, HR in Dodgers' Series Win Over Cubs**

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Shohei Ohtani: 3-for-3, HR, walk, 2 runs in Sunday's Dodgers 6-0 win over the Cubs. First homer in 13 games. A gentle reminder that the most dangerous hitter in baseball was just taking a brief nap. #Dodgers #LetsGoDodgers

stories.title: **Mets Managerial Situation — Mendoza Under Fire as Team Battles Back**

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text: The Mets entered 2026 with the 3rd-highest playoff odds in baseball (79.5%). A 12-game losing streak dropped them to 7-16. Carlos Mendoza's seat is reportedly hot. The Red Sox already fired their manager. Who's next? #Mets #MLB

stories.title: **Monday's MLB Recap — Torkelson Shines, Murakami Watch Continues**

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text: MLB standings Tuesday morning: Braves first to 20 wins. Dodgers 19-9. Yankees 18-10. Padres 18-10. NL Central: all 5 teams still over .500. Murakami and Alvarez tied at 11 HRs apiece. Great month of baseball so far. #MLB
